description:
You are an L-1 IT support technician who has been asked to deliver a quick configuration-file
    audit for your team.  
    
    Existing data structure  
      • All configuration backups are stored in  
        /home/user/config_backups/  
        (this directory already contains several “.conf” files – some are in nested
        sub-directories).  
      • A writeable directory for the report already exists at  
        /home/user/audit/  

    Your assignment  
      1. Generate a plain-text report called  
         /home/user/audit/config_line_report.txt  
         (create/overwrite as needed).  
      2. The report must list the number of text lines in every “.conf” file that exists
         anywhere under /home/user/config_backups/.  
         • One line in the report per file.  
         • Each line must follow *exactly* this format (no extra spaces or characters):  
           <integer_line_count><single_space><absolute_path_to_the_file>  
           Example (illustrative only):  
           42 /home/user/config_backups/example.conf  
      3. Produce the report using **one single pipeline** composed of a `find` command
         and an `xargs` command (plus any standard Unix utilities you wish to add on
         either end of that same pipeline).  
         • No intermediate files are allowed.  
         • You may not run more than one `find` command.  
         • The entire solution must be executed from a single terminal prompt.  

    Verification notes  
      • The automated checker will confirm that  
          – /home/user/audit/config_line_report.txt exists,  
          – every “.conf” file under /home/user/config_backups/ (including files in
            sub-directories) appears exactly once in the report,  
          – each listed line count is correct,  
          – each line matches the required “<count><space><absolute_path>” pattern, and  
          – no extra lines or characters are present.  
      • The order of the lines in the report does not matter.  

    Remember: only one pipeline containing *exactly one* `find` and *exactly one* `xargs`
    should be issued from the terminal to create the report.
truth:
Pre-existing directory and files (all already present before the agent starts):

    /home/user/config_backups/sshd.conf
        Line 1: # sshd configuration
        Line 2: Port 22
        Line 3: PermitRootLogin no
        Line 4: PasswordAuthentication yes
        Line 5: ChallengeResponseAuthentication no
        (5 lines total)

    /home/user/config_backups/nginx.conf
        Line 1: # nginx configuration
        Line 2: user www-data;
        Line 3: worker_processes auto;
        Line 4: pid /run/nginx.pid;
        Line 5: events { worker_connections 768; }
        Line 6: http { 
        Line 7:     include       mime.types;
        Line 8:     default_type  application/octet-stream;
        Line 9:     sendfile        on;
        (9 lines total)

    /home/user/config_backups/app.conf
        Line 1: # application configuration
        Line 2: debug=false
        Line 3: port=8080
        Line 4: log_level=info
        (4 lines total)

    /home/user/config_backups/old/legacy.conf
        Line 1: # legacy configuration
        Line 2: feature_x=disabled
        Line 3: safe_mode=true
        (3 lines total)

    /home/user/config_backups/readme.txt
        (2 lines – not a “.conf”, should be ignored)

    Pre-made, writeable directory for the report:
        /home/user/audit/        (empty at start)

    Expected final file created by the agent:
        /home/user/audit/config_line_report.txt
            Must contain exactly the four lines below (order unimportant):

            5 /home/user/config_backups/sshd.conf
            9 /home/user/config_backups/nginx.conf
            4 /home/user/config_backups/app.conf
            3 /home/user/config_backups/old/legacy.conf
